description: Catalog of AI tools + where their history/cache lives. Vanish does NOT auto-delete — it tells you where to find each location and gives you the exact command to run yourself. This avoids accidentally wiping important data.

categories.local-app: Desktop app or IDE with local cache files
categories.web-ui: Web-only service — deletion via account settings

preCheck: Close Cursor before running deletion commands
Export important conversations first: Cursor → Settings → Export conversations
workspaceStorage contains project-specific AI context — delete with care
cursor.notes: Cursor stores AI chat history + code context + completion cache locally. These paths cover local-only data. Cloud-synced account data requires separate deletion at cursor.com/settings.

preCheck: Close VS Code before running deletion
Do NOT delete 'extensions' folder unless you want to re-install everything
workspaceStorage contains per-project AI completion telemetry — deleting wipes it for all projects
vscode-copilot.notes: Copilot logs its queries to local telemetry before sending to GitHub. Deletion wipes the local logs but doesn't affect GitHub's server-side telemetry (opt-out that separately via github.com/settings/copilot).

preCheck: Close Claude Desktop app before deletion
IndexedDB / Local Storage contain auth tokens — deleting will sign you out
Conversation history itself lives on Anthropic's servers — delete via claude.ai/chat settings
claude-desktop.notes: Claude Desktop caches conversation drafts + auth state locally. Actual conversation history is server-side — use claude.ai settings for that.

preCheck: Conversations themselves are server-side — use chatgpt.com settings for that
chatgpt-desktop.notes: ChatGPT Desktop caches local session + logs. Your actual conversations live in OpenAI's servers.

walkthrough: Click your profile (bottom-left) → Settings → Data controls
Click 'Export data' — save the .zip backup somewhere safe
Then: 'Delete all chats' (wipes conversation history but keeps account) OR 'Delete account' (wipes everything)
chatgpt-web.verification: Sidebar becomes empty; confirmation email arrives within 30 min. Account deletion processes over 30 days.
chatgpt-web.notes: If you only want to hide past chats from training but keep them visible: Settings → Data Controls → 'Improve the model for everyone' → OFF (covered by `vanish ai-opt-out --chatgpt`).

walkthrough: Scroll to 'Delete account' (or select individual conversations → delete)
claude-web.verification: Conversations disappear from sidebar; account deletion processes within 30 days.
claude-web.notes: Anthropic doesn't train on consumer chat by default, so this is purely about removing YOUR copy of the conversation — not training data.

walkthrough: Choose 'All time' to delete everything, OR 'Last day/week/month' for partial
gemini-activity.verification: 'Your data' log shows no recent activity. Deletion is immediate but may take 24h to propagate.
gemini-activity.notes: Also set auto-delete: click the 'Auto-delete activity older than' dropdown → 3 months (shortest). Turning off 'Gemini Apps Activity' also stops future retention.

walkthrough: Scroll to 'Threads' → click 'Delete all threads' (or delete individually from sidebar)
Also toggle 'AI Data Retention' OFF under Privacy if not already
perplexity.verification: Library becomes empty. Account data deletion processes per their retention policy.
perplexity.notes: Pro users have different retention settings — check if you're Pro. Also covered by `vanish ai-opt-out --perplexity` for the training toggle.

walkthrough: Click 'Delete my documents' — this wipes all drafts/edits you've made via Grammarly
Separately, go to 'Subscription' → 'Delete account' if you want full removal
grammarly.verification: Document list becomes empty in dashboard.
grammarly.notes: Grammarly retains your writing samples for model improvement by default. Separate from history deletion: `vanish ai-opt-out --grammarly` toggles the training setting.
